title: `Cannot connect to Podman` error
body:
### Issue Description

I'm running podman via wsl on win11.

I started getting `Cannot connect to Podman.` issue where basically nothing works: `podman version`, `podman compose ...`, etc. I tried reinstalling podman, wsl, rebooting a bunch of times but nothing helps. With the last reinstall, everything seemed fine: intsalled cli, run `machine init`, `machine start` - all good. Then run my compose file with `compose up`, and after a min or so the issue came back.

For instance:
```ps
PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman compose up db
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49853: connectex: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond.
``` 

Output of `podman system connection list`:
```ps
PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity                                                        Default     ReadWrite
podman-machine-default       ssh://user@127.0.0.1:49853/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\aliser\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  true        true    
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@127.0.0.1:49853/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\aliser\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  false       true    
```

`podman machine init`:
```ps
PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman machine init
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists
```

`podman machine start`:
```ps
PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: machine podman-machine-default: VM already running or starting
```

`podman machine start` after `podman machine stop`:
```ps
PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port
```

Setting machine as rootful didn't help - same error as above (without the rootless mode part).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman cli on win11.
2. Run init commands: `podman machine init`, `podman machine start`.
3. Run `podman compose up`.


### Describe the results you received

`Cannot connect to Podman.` error on every command.

### Describe the results you expected

No error.

### podman info output

```yaml
`podman info`

PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49853: connectex: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond.


`podman version`

PS C:\Users\aliser\Desktop\repos\chat-app> podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49853: connectex: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I'm running a single db container via compose locally.

I don't have any VPN or web server running. I don't have docker installed.

### Additional information

-

comments.body:
Fixed by removing `networkingMode=mirrored` from `.wslconfig` in my windows user dir. 

I was trying to fix connectivity issues with a web server that I tried to install inside wsl and one recommendation was to put that line in the wsl config. So removing it fixed my issue.

Would be great if there was some kind of warning message that this can be the case because I can see multiple related issues regarding this setting, such as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22240.
